title: Disco Elysium
edition_pinned: The Final Cut, March 2021 release era — pins full English voice-over, political vision quests, added characters and area, controller support, and UI changes.
moat: Skills speak as characters, turning stat allocation into control over which thoughts enter dialogue.

loop_skeleton.description: Inspect Martinaise, question a person, receive skill interjections, choose what to say, resolve checks, then spend points on skills or thoughts before returning to blocked leads.

depth_mechanisms.name: skills as interlocutors
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Twenty-four skills have voices; passive checks insert observations and arguments, and some unlock lines the player may speak or suppress.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Replace passive skill speech with bonus labels: two builds facing the same conversation lose build-dependent lines and the player loses the choice to repeat or suppress an idea.

depth_mechanisms.name: failure continuation
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Dice checks can fail into authored outcomes instead of ending every route; some checks reopen after a skill or thought changes.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Replace failed checks with a refusal and reload prompt: the count of scenes reachable only through a failed roll becomes zero and failure stops generating role-play state.

depth_mechanisms.name: thought internalization
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Discovered Thoughts occupy slots, take in-world time to internalize, apply effects, unlock topics, and cost skill points to forget or expand capacity.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Remove slot limits, time, and forgetting costs: players never trade a skill point or cabinet slot between identities, so thought curation decisions fall to zero.

depth_mechanisms.name: build-gated perception
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Attribute and skill allocation opens some observations and solutions while closing others, so a build changes what the detective can notice, infer, endure, or say.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: Equalize all skill values for two playthroughs: at matched conversations and objects, the set of skill-gated observations and solutions stops differing by build.

feel_signature: Hear parts of the detective argue, decide which impulse becomes speech, then accept a dice result that may expose another route or another failure.
second_session_answer: Return with another attribute spread, signature skill, and Thought Cabinet; the same district supplies different voices, clues, failures, and political commitments.
packaging_lessons: Ship one conversation where two skill builds receive different interjections and can act on or suppress them before expanding the district.
Treat failure as authored content: every shipped check needs a consequence, a continuation, or a route to retry rather than a reload dependency.
anti_lessons: Do not copy the volume of text without build-conditioned delivery; prose that reaches every build in the same order does not reproduce the mechanism.
Do not make every skill increase an upgrade; high skill values can interrupt, mislead, or push behavior, which keeps allocation from becoming one-way optimization.

system_bom.note: Exploration feeds conversations; conversations feed checks, thoughts, tasks, and reasons to revisit people or places.
Twenty-four skills control check odds, passive interjections, dialogue options, perception, and the voices addressing the player.
A scrolling dialogue reel presents speaker text, skill voices, check odds, and response choices in one reading surface.
Thoughts can direct a return to a person with another topic, and political vision quests add dialogue routes.
Experience becomes skill points spent on skill ranks, Thought slots, or forgetting a Thought, tying growth to dialogue access and identity.
Clothing and tools alter skills or enable interactions, letting the player change a check setup through carried objects.
Health can end the investigation, while failed dice outcomes can continue into authored scenes.

citations.claim: Edition pin: The Final Cut added full voice acting, political quests, an area, characters, controller support, and UI changes in its 2021 release.
citations.source: ZA/UM, 'Disco Elysium – The Final Cut coming to PS4 and PS5 in 2021,' PlayStation Blog, https://blog.playstation.com/2020/12/10/disco-elysium-the-final-cut-coming-to-ps4-and-ps5-in-2021/; ZA/UM, 'Jamais Vu Update,' https://discoelysium.com/devblog/2022/02/07/jamais-vu-update-huge-thanks-for-2021

citations.claim: Moat: skills speak to the player, passive checks form ideas, and the player may voice or suppress those ideas.
Depth — skills as interlocutors: passive skills deliver observations, converse with one another, and can add dialogue choices.
citations.source: ZA/UM, 'On skill checks,' https://discoelysium.com/devblog/2016/09/19/on-skill-checks; Robert Kurvitz interview, 'The Guts of the Game,' https://discoelysium.com/devblog/2017/08/07/the-guts-of-the-game

citations.claim: Depth — failure continuation: big moments use dice and the team authored outcomes so failed rolls can produce play rather than require success.
citations.source: ZA/UM, 'Disco Elysium – The Final Cut coming to PS4 and PS5 in 2021,' PlayStation Blog, https://blog.playstation.com/2020/12/10/disco-elysium-the-final-cut-coming-to-ps4-and-ps5-in-2021/

citations.claim: Depth — thought internalization: Thoughts use slots and in-world time, can unlock return topics, and cost skill points to forget or expand capacity.
Depth — build-gated perception: skill levels open and close options; Visual Calculus, Empathy, Suggestion, and Physical Instrument expose different evidence and routes.
citations.source: Chris Priestman, ZA/UM, 'What kind of detective to become in Disco Elysium – The Final Cut,' PlayStation Blog, https://blog.playstation.com/2021/03/29/what-kind-of-detective-to-become-in-disco-elysium-the-final-cut/

citations.claim: Feel signature: twenty-four skill personalities address the player, while dice decide moments and failures have authored outcomes.
Packaging lesson — build-conditioned conversation: passive checks generate observations and optional lines according to skill level.
Packaging lesson — authored failure: ZA/UM says dice determine moments and failed outcomes were written to remain playable.
BOM — play-loop and quests: conversations discover Thoughts; some Thoughts direct the player back to a person with another topic, and Final Cut adds political quests.
citations.source: ZA/UM, Thought Cabinet post, https://discoelysium.com/devblog/2019/09/30/introducing-the-thought-cabinet; Final Cut announcement, https://blog.playstation.com/2020/12/10/disco-elysium-the-final-cut-coming-to-ps4-and-ps5-in-2021/

citations.claim: BOM — skills: the game has twenty-four skills across four attributes; levels change proficiency, solutions, perception, and dialogue access.
citations.source: ZA/UM, Final Cut announcement, https://blog.playstation.com/2020/12/10/disco-elysium-the-final-cut-coming-to-ps4-and-ps5-in-2021/; ZA/UM build guide, https://blog.playstation.com/2021/03/29/what-kind-of-detective-to-become-in-disco-elysium-the-final-cut/

citations.claim: BOM — UI: ZA/UM's skill-check post shows the dialogue engine using animation cues and a dialogue surface for passive interjections and player lines.
BOM — progression: skill points improve skills, open Thought slots, or remove Thoughts; Thoughts take in-world time and apply effects.
BOM — inventory: the Final Cut guide documents clothing bonuses that modify Drama and support the autopsy, plus equipment changes before checks.
citations.source: Chris Priestman, ZA/UM, 'How to become a superstar detective in Disco Elysium – The Final Cut,' PlayStation Blog, https://blog.playstation.com/?p=347308

citations.claim: BOM — win-loss-death: Physique grants health and can prevent death, while authored failed dice outcomes continue into scenes.
citations.source: ZA/UM build guide, https://blog.playstation.com/2021/03/29/what-kind-of-detective-to-become-in-disco-elysium-the-final-cut/; ZA/UM Final Cut announcement, https://blog.playstation.com/2020/12/10/disco-elysium-the-final-cut-coming-to-ps4-and-ps5-in-2021/

citations.claim: Anti-lesson — text-volume inference: passive checks and skill-gated lines make delivery depend on the build, so equal delivery across builds does not reproduce the mechanism.
Anti-lesson — high-skill interruption: ZA/UM describes skills as personalities that converse, interrupt, and offer ideas the player can voice or suppress.
